title: Admin agent configuration and email identity
objective: Let administrators safely manage prompts and bounded runtime parameters for every user-facing Agent, allow password login by username or registered email, let legacy username-only accounts bind a verified email, and grant the designated email account administrator access.

in: Username or registered-email password login in sparrow-user
Verified email binding for authenticated users without an email
Idempotent promotion of the environment-configured administrator email without storing a password
Service-owned Agent prompt and bounded runtime parameter persistence in sparrow-ai and sparrow-industry-chain
Server-side admin authorization, audit records and a unified frontend Agent management page
Additive database migrations, focused tests, frontend build and harness verification
out: Creating or resetting the designated administrator password
Moving business-service configuration into a shared database
Exposing model API keys or decrypted secrets to the browser
Replacing the existing industry-chain model provider configuration page

acceptanceCriteria.description: Password login accepts either a username or an already-bound email and preserves generic credential errors.
evidence: UserService passwordLoginAcceptsRegisteredEmail test passes and the password endpoint retains the legacy username request alias.

acceptanceCriteria.description: An authenticated account without email can bind a uniquely owned email only after a purpose-specific verification code succeeds.
evidence: UserService tests verify the bind-only Redis code namespace, unique ownership checks, atomic user update and code deletion after success.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The account bound to the environment-configured administrator email receives the admin role through idempotent startup and runtime paths without a committed account value, password or token.
evidence: Startup migration, email registration and verified binding promote the environment-configured identity; tracked files contain no administrator account value, password or token.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Every user-facing Agent has a service-owned persisted prompt/config profile, admin-only CRUD and audit, and runtime execution consumes the active values with safe defaults.
evidence: sparrow-ai and sparrow-industry-chain own separate Agent configuration/audit tables and admin APIs.
Focused service tests verify admin authorization and audited updates; runtime chat/research tests pass with persisted profiles and safe defaults.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Administrators can manage all Agent profiles from one page, while non-admin routes remain blocked and users without email can reach the binding flow.
evidence: Frontend production build includes AgentConfigView and BindEmailModal.
Browser QA verifies username-or-email password input, no console errors, and unauthenticated /admin/agents redirect to the home page.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Additive migrations, focused backend tests, frontend build and repository guards pass without overwriting pre-existing user changes.
evidence: node tools/harness.mjs changed passed both guards, all seven backend modules with 142 tests, and the frontend production build.
Both schema changes have idempotent migration scripts and git diff --check reports no whitespace errors.

steps.description: Inventory authentication, role, Agent prompts/configuration and frontend administration routes.
steps.verification: Existing user, AI, industry-chain and frontend implementations inspected; pre-existing changes recorded.

steps.description: Implement email-aware password login, verified email binding and designated administrator promotion.
steps.verification: sparrow-user and shared tests passed (51 total); additive user migration reviewed.

steps.description: Implement service-owned Agent prompt/config persistence, admin APIs, audit and runtime consumption.
steps.verification: sparrow-ai 24 tests and sparrow-industry-chain 40 tests passed with shared tests.

steps.description: Build unified Agent administration and verified email-binding frontend flows.
steps.verification: Vite production build passed; browser verified password identifier UI and admin route guard without console errors.

steps.description: Document ownership and operations, run full change-aware verification and record evidence.
steps.verification: node tools/harness.mjs changed passed both guards, 142 backend tests and the frontend production build.

decisions: sparrow-user remains authoritative for identity and roles; downstream services authorize administrators through internal profile calls.
sparrow-ai and sparrow-industry-chain each own their Agent configuration and audit tables; the frontend aggregates their public admin APIs.
Verification codes are purpose-scoped so a login code cannot be replayed to bind an email.
The designated administrator identity is read only from SPARROW_ADMIN_EMAIL; the repository contains no account value, password, token or API key.
Authenticated live API, SMTP delivery and the populated administrator page remain deployment smoke checks because local backend infrastructure was not running.

commands.result: PASS: shared 39, sparrow-ai 24 and sparrow-industry-chain 40 tests.
PASS: username-or-email input visible, no console errors, unauthenticated admin route redirected.
PASS: no whitespace errors; line-ending normalization warnings only.
PASS: both guards, full seven-module backend reactor with 142 tests, and frontend production build.

commands.command: git grep plus repository scan for the administrator account value
commands.result: PASS: zero tracked or non-.env repository hits; the ignored root .env contains SPARROW_ADMIN_EMAIL.
PASS: Compose resolved the required SPARROW_ADMIN_EMAIL from the root .env.
PASS: shared 39 and sparrow-user 12 tests after removing the repository default.

nextAction: Apply the two additive migrations in deployment, sign in with the designated email, then smoke-test email binding and one audited Agent update.
